title: pavetta wallichiana
intro: Pavetta wallichiana, also known as the Indian rose or the Bengal rose, is a flowering plant species in the family Rubiaceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and flowers of the plant are used to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and wounds, due to its medicinal properties, which provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
